title: Rostraria cristata
intro: Rostraria cristata, also known as Restharrow or Sharp-leaved Restharrow, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the family Poaceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and sharp, pointed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and digestive system support. Rostraria cristata contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Rostraria cristata is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea form, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and poultices for skin conditions and injuries.
taxonomy: Rostraria cristata is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Rostraria, which is a small group of grasses native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of monocotyledonous flowering plants commonly known as grasses. The Poaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, each with distinct characteristics and growth habits.
